div1.entryFree:
Hobby-horse, 1) one of the principal parts played in the ancient morris-dance, but of late growing out of use: the h. is forgot, LLL III, 30. Hml. III, 2, 142. Hml. III, 2, 142
2) a term of contempt for a loose and frivolous person, either male or female: which these --s must not hear, Ado III, 2, 75. callest thou my love h.? LLL III, 31. my wife's a h. Wint. I, 2, 276 (O. Edd. holy-horse). give it your h. Oth. IV, 1, 160.
